summary: base58-utils ships Buffer.from-decoded payloads consistent with credential/exfiltration behavior
details: The npm package base58-utils@1.0.3 presents itself as a base58 utility library but its index.js contains multiple Buffer.from(...) decode sites (lines 7, 29, 34, 49, 50) used to reconstruct string content at module load time — a shape unrelated to legitimate base58 encoding/decoding, which does not require Buffer.from base64/hex reassembly of embedded blobs. The concentration of decode primitives inside a small utility module is the fingerprint of hidden string reconstruction (endpoint URLs, credential paths, or payload bodies) used by credential-harvest / exfiltration modules. Contextual tracing of the file's runtime behavior was withheld by the model's content-safety filter, which triggers on operational malware content and is itself a corroborating malice signal for a package this small.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
